retrieved_docs.title: Lensometry -- Technique or Treatment -- Measurement of Cylindrical Power
retrieved_docs.content: To compensate for a cylindrical error, note the cylindrical value that is the null point of the equipment. If the null values of the equipment are 15º and 195º, the lower value must be subtracted from any eventual axis read. For example, If the lowest null value is 15º, and the measurement yields a finding of -0.75 DS - 0.50 DC x 145, the final prescription will be -0.75 DS - 0.50 DC x 130.

retrieved_docs.title: Intraocular Pressure -- Definition/Introduction -- Tonometry
retrieved_docs.content: Limitations exist in applanation technology due to reliance on the Imbert-Fick principle, which presumes that pressure within a sphere equals the force necessary to flatten its surface divided by the area flattened. [25] This principle does not consider the inherent rigidity or biomechanical properties of the corneal wall. The law applies to perfectly spherical solids that are flexible, dry, and infinitely thin, which do not reflect the anatomy of the eye. [26] The force of capillary attraction of the tear meniscus opposes corneal rigidity when the flattened area is 3.06 mm in diameter.

retrieved_docs.title: Jackson Cross Cylinder -- Clinical Significance -- Determining/Refining the Astigmatic Axis
retrieved_docs.content: The degree of rotation while refining the axis is dependent on the power of the cylinder and the quality of response from the patient. After the initial axis change, the degree of subsequent axis changes is dependent on the magnitude of the initial change and the quality of the response.

retrieved_docs.title: The Spherical Equivalent -- Contraindications
retrieved_docs.content: Spherical equivalent prescriptions are also contraindicated for correcting moderate to high oblique astigmatic errors, even amongst adults.

retrieved_docs.title: The Conoid of Sturm -- Function -- The Spherical Equivalent of a Toric Lens
retrieved_docs.content: The spherical equivalent of a toric lens is its average focusing power. It is the average of the 2 meridional powers (optical cross format) or can be calculated by adding half the denominator (designated DC) to the numerator (designated DS) when using the fraction format. For example, the spherical equivalent of the lens discussed above would be: +4 DS + 1/2(+2DC) = +4 + 1 = +5 diopter. The position of the circle of most minor confusion of the conoid of Sturm can be found by calculating the focal distance of the spherical equivalent power of the astigmatic lens.

retrieved_docs.title: The Spherical Equivalent -- Contraindications
retrieved_docs.content: Conditions that precipitate visual impairment via irregular astigmatism require consideration of their cylindrical errors for rigid contact lens fitting. This includes individuals with clinically evident corneal ectasia, as well as subclinical cases that are only observed via keratometry or keratography. [38]

retrieved_docs.title: Toric Intraocular Lenses -- Preparation -- Spherical Power Calculation
retrieved_docs.content: Intraocular lens power calculation should consider the posterior corneal curvature, effective lens position, and surgically induced astigmatism. [43] Axial length measurement is undertaken using ultrasonic or optical measurement systems. Keratometry to assess the corneal curvatures and astigmatism can be done using manual and automated keratometry. They may include slit scanning systems, optical coherence tomography-based systems, Scheimpflug imaging systems, Placido-based topographers, and an aberrometer. The various measurements are done on two different devices with different principles, and results cross verified for accuracy. Intraoperative wavefront aberrometry rapidly replaces all these calculators to determine the power and axis. The Barrett toric calculator has better predictability since it considers both estimated lens position and posterior corneal power. [44] [45]

retrieved_docs.title: Jackson Cross Cylinder -- Clinical Significance
retrieved_docs.content: The JCC technique of refining the power and axis of a cylindrical prescription is one of the subjective techniques used in refracting a patient with astigmatism. [23]

retrieved_docs.title: Phacoemulsification -- Equipment
retrieved_docs.content: These crystals' two and fro motion produces the mechanical energy for emulsifying the nucleus. The stroke length of the handpiece depends on the power of the machine. The to and fro motion results in compression, producing gas bubbles that help disassemble the nucleus. This process is known as cavitation. The vibrations of the tip can be longitudinal or torsional, which aid in the mechanical disassembly of the nucleus. [26]

retrieved_docs.content: Other procedures inducing or assisting delivery is a broad obstetric category that encompasses the specific outcome of فشل التوليد بالملقط (forceps delivery failure). Within this framework, الولادة بالملقط (forceps delivery) is directly linked to فشل التوليد بالملقط, indicating that the failure represents a particular adverse event or subcategory of the forceps delivery procedure. Consequently, both الولادة بالملقط and its associated failure, فشل التوليد بالملقط, are situated under the larger grouping of other procedures that induce or assist delivery, highlighting their interrelated roles in assisted vaginal birth and the importance of recognizing potential complications.
